title: I Made $500K Trading Small-Cap Stocks  Have You Seen This Strategy?
author: Zealousideal-Hat4801
selftext:
I do not come from a financial background  in fact, I started out as a logger.

The long years spent in the forest taught me a very different kind of discipline: patience, perseverance, and a deep reverence for risk. You cannot rush the process, nor can you ignore any warning signs  for a single mistake can lead to grave consequences.

When I eventually transitioned into the financial markets, I realized that these principles remained just as applicable.

I devoted a significant amount of time to studying small-cap stocks, and as the years went by, one thing became increasingly clear: most violent price swings do not occur randomly—they follow specific structures.

I no longer blindly chased hot trends or acted on impulse; instead, I shifted my focus to identifying repeatable patterns that emerge just before a strong trend takes off.

This is the framework I have consistently used to uncover small- and mid-cap stocks with breakout potential, while managing risk throughout the entire trading process:

Trend Screening:  Price > 200-Day Moving Average (MA 200): Trade only stocks that are in a long-term bull market. 50-Day Moving Average (MA 50) is trending upward: This ensures positive medium-term momentum (thereby avoiding the trap of "catching a falling knife").

Momentum Accumulation:  Flat Base: The price oscillates sideways within a narrow range (indicating a phase of accumulation). Volume Dry-up: Look for signs of shrinking volume during the base-building process, which indicates that selling pressure has largely exhausted itself.

Entry Trigger:  Entry Point: Buy when the daily or weekly closing price breaks above—and holds firmly above—a specific resistance level. Momentum Test: Volume: Volume must surge to 1.5 to 2 times the average volume to confirm the presence of institutional buying interest. RSI (Relative Strength Index): The ideal range is between 40 and 70. If RSI > 80, avoid entering the trade (as it suggests the stock is already in an overbought state).

Exit Defense:  Stop-Loss Setting: Set your stop-loss level below the 50-day moving average, or below the recent low point of the base-building pattern. Trailing Win: Maintain a position in alignment with the prevailing trend, using the 20-day Moving Average (MA 20) as a guide for holding; close the position \*only\* when the price falls below this moving average.

In the long run, the key lies in consistently executing the correct operational procedures. I have compiled my specific configuration settings into a dedicated folder and am sharing them completely free of charge with anyone who needs them. Creating this set of configurations was no small feat, so let's keep up the hard work!

comments.author: Background-Syrup-626
comments.body: I'd like to use your folder as a reference; could you please send it to me? Thank you.

replies.author: Zealousideal-Hat4801
replies.body: It might not be suitable for everyone, but it is free, so you can use it as a reference.
